decks.notes: The deck focuses on fast-paced aggressive gameplay, utilizing cheap spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It has a strong emphasis on instants and sorceries, with cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics providing utility and disruption. The goal is to pressure the opponent early on with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage while using burn spells like Fiery Impulse to maintain board control. The deck aims to win by overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of spells and creatures before they can stabilize.

The main idea of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to establish board presence quickly with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub, Skyclave Apparition, and Aven Interrupter, supported by mana ramp from Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves. Cards like Collected Company allow you to dig for more threats while generating value. Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupt opponents' strategies and slow them down, giving you time to overwhelm them with your aggressive creatures. Surrak, Elusive Hunter and Elspeth, Storm Slayer provide additional power and versatility to close out the game.

The deck focuses on combining the abilities of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm opponents. By utilizing cards like Ral Zarek, Fatal Push, and Thoughtseize for control and removal, while also including defensive creatures like Iron-Shield Elf and Guardian of New Benalia, the deck aims to protect its combo pieces until it can assemble the winning combination. The key strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up the Greasefang-Parhelion combo for a game-ending attack.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish a board presence and deal damage to the opponent efficiently. By utilizing cheap spells and creatures with prowess abilities, the deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain it throughout the game. The inclusion of cards like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage allows the deck to both control the opponent's board and push through for more damage. The synergy between the spells and creatures in this deck makes it well-suited for aggressive plays and fast-paced gameplay.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate massive value and overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes cards like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance to support the combo while controlling the board with removal spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The deck also includes utility lands like Geier Reach Sanitarium and Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire to provide additional advantages. Overall, the deck aims to quickly set up its combo pieces and outvalue the opponent to secure victory.

This deck focuses on utilizing spells to fill the graveyard quickly, allowing for the reanimation of Arclight Phoenix multiple times throughout the game. The deck aims to be proactive and aggressive, putting pressure on the opponent with a combination of cheap spells, card draw, and the powerful flying threat of Arclight Phoenix. By efficiently cycling through the deck and utilizing cards like Traumatic Critique and Vibrant Outburst to trigger the Phoenix's ability, the deck can overwhelm opponents and close out games quickly.

The deck focuses on playing cheap and efficient creatures to apply pressure on the opponent early in the game. By utilizing cards like Archon of Emeria, Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, and Skyclave Apparition to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a board presence with creatures like Llanowar Elves and Badgermole Cub, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays. The inclusion of cards like Collected Company and Elspeth, Storm Slayer offers the deck additional ways to generate value and close out the game quickly.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game by disrupting your opponent's strategy with cards like Duress and Thoughtseize, while also utilizing removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay. Additionally, the deck aims to generate card advantage and value through cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Professor Dellian Fel. The deck also includes powerful threats like Graveyard Trespasser and Witherbloom Charm to pressure the opponent and finish the game. Overall, the deck focuses on maintaining control of the game while setting up its own win conditions.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by disrupting opponents' strategies with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, while also utilizing efficient removal spells like Abrupt Decay. The deck aims to establish a dominant board presence with creatures like Badgermole Cub and Emeritus of Abundance, while also generating value with cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck also has a subtheme of ramping and fixing mana with cards like Llanowar Elves and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth. Overall, the deck seeks to outvalue and outlast opponents in a grindy, attrition-based matchup.

The deck is focused on controlling and manipulating the game through counterspells, removal, and card draw. It utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and a companion like Yorion, Sky Nomad to generate value and outvalue opponents. There are also several win conditions in the deck like The Wandering Emperor and Ultima. The mana base is built for consistency and to support the control game plan. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt opponents' strategies while setting up its own win conditions efficiently.

The deck revolves around assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate a significant advantage. The main strategy involves using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's game plan and ensure a clear path for the combo to come together. Monument to Endurance provides additional support by protecting key pieces of the combo, while Vanishing Verse and Erode offer removal options to deal with opposing threats. The deck aims to establish a dominant position on the battlefield through its synergistic interactions and proactive game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by using a combination of efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, along with creatures like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The deck also utilizes ramp with cards like Llanowar Elves and Mutavault to quickly gain mana advantage. Sheoldred, the Apocalypse serves as a powerful finisher, while cards like Thoughtseize and Culling Ritual help disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to outvalue opponents through control and attrition, ultimately leading to victory.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and disruption. By including cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while also putting pressure on them with creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Elvish Mystic. Collected Company provides card advantage and helps to quickly flood the board with threats. The deck's mana base is streamlined for consistency, ensuring that it can deploy its threats quickly and efficiently in order to win the game fast.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly apply pressure on the opponent with low-cost, aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, supported by spells like Fiery Impulse and Flow State to control the board. The deck also includes card draw options like Sleight of Hand and Consider to maintain hand advantage. The focus is on dealing damage swiftly and efficiently, utilizing the deck's synergy and versatile card options to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on utilizing cheap and efficient spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures. By combining prowess triggers with burn spells and card draw, it aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while controlling the board. The deck also includes mana-fixing lands to ensure consistency in casting its spells. The key strategy is to maintain a fast pace and constantly apply pressure, leaving the opponent with limited options to stabilize the board.

This deck focuses on utilizing powerful control tools to disrupt the opponent while efficiently ramping and generating value. By combining powerful removal in cards like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push with threats like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, the deck aims to control the game and slowly grind out advantages over the opponent. The inclusion of mana ramp and fixing tools like Mutavault and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth allows for smooth mana development, enabling the player to both disrupt the opponent and establish a strong board presence.
